activities_projects.title: Work Calculation with Angled Force
activities_projects.objective: Compute work done when force is applied at an angle
steps: Apply a known force at an angle to move an object
Measure the distance moved
Calculate work done using W = F × d × cosθ
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands effect of force direction on work

activities_projects.title: Comparing Work Done by Different Forces
activities_projects.objective: Analyze work done by different magnitudes of force
steps: Move the same object using different forces
Measure distance each time
Calculate work done in each case and compare
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects force magnitude and work done

activities_projects.title: Energy Transformation in Pendulum
activities_projects.objective: Observe potential to kinetic energy conversion
steps: Pull pendulum to a certain height and release
Observe speed at lowest point
Explain energy transformation from PE to KE

activities_projects.title: Kinetic Energy Calculation
activities_projects.objective: Calculate kinetic energy for different masses and speeds
steps: Push toy car at different speeds and with different masses
Measure speed using stopwatch and track
Calculate kinetic energy using KE = 1/2 mv^2
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes effect of mass and velocity on KE

activities_projects.title: Potential Energy at Heights
activities_projects.objective: Calculate potential energy at varying heights
steps: Lift weight to different heights
Record mass and height
Calculate potential energy using PE = mgh and compare
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands relationship between height, mass, and PE

activities_projects.title: Efficiency of Simple Machines
activities_projects.objective: Calculate work input and output to find efficiency
steps: Use simple machine to lift weight
Measure effort and distance moved (input work)
Measure load lifted and height (output work)
Calculate efficiency = (Output work/Input work) × 100%

activities_projects.title: Work Done Against Friction
steps: Pull block across rough surface with known force
Measure distance moved
Calculate work done to overcome friction using W = F × d

activities_projects.title: Energy Loss in Real Systems
steps: Roll a ball down a ramp onto different surfaces
Observe final height or speed
Explain loss of mechanical energy due to friction or air resistance

activities_projects.title: Comparing Kinetic and Potential Energy
activities_projects.objective: Relate KE and PE at different positions
steps: Release pendulum or car from height
Record speed at different points
Calculate KE and PE and verify total mechanical energy remains approximately constant
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands conservation of mechanical energy concept

activities_projects.title: Work Done in Lifting Objects
activities_projects.objective: Calculate work done when lifting objects vertically
steps: Lift weights of different masses to various heights
Record mass and height
Calculate work done using W = mgh
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes effect of mass and height on work done
